intended_command_or_script: planned scripts/source_capture.ps1 or scripts/source_capture.py reads a TSV queue row and refuses missing source_id/url/accessed_at/capture_method
output_locations: data/source_snapshots/YYYY-MM-DD/ for HTML/text/JSON; assets/source_snapshots/YYYY-MM-DD/ for screenshots/content intended as visual evidence
required_hashes: sha256 for every committed capture note or lightweight file; sha256 note for ignored heavy media
git_safety: Queue and logs are committed; large binaries stay ignored unless explicitly allowed
robots_rights_note: Record robots/terms/rights note before capture; private/account-specific pages marked console-limited
validation_gate: Queue row validates required fields and target path before any network/browser work.

intended_command_or_script: planned script downloads or records local PDF path, hashes it, and emits metadata without committing heavyweight PDFs by default
output_locations: data/source_snapshots/YYYY-MM-DD/ capture note plus ignored assets/source_media/YYYY-MM-DD/ optional local PDF path
required_hashes: sha256 of PDF or capture note; optional page-render hashes for selected pages
ledger_updates: source_snapshot_protocol.tsv row; sources.tsv note; assets_manifest.tsv only for rendered page/screenshot assets
git_safety: *.pdf remains ignored except explicitly allowlisted text/markdown; commit note/hash not binary
robots_rights_note: Prefer official source URLs; record license/terms/fair-use plan for any page render
validation_gate: Hash-only row is acceptable if PDF is local and ignored; page refs required before figure extraction.

intended_command_or_script: planned script fetches HTML/text when allowed; if blocked, writes capture-note with status and fallback route
ledger_updates: source_snapshot_protocol.tsv row; sources.tsv note only if claim-ready
git_safety: Commit lightweight HTML/text if size is reasonable; do not commit browser cache or profile
robots_rights_note: Record HTTP status, redirects, robots/terms observation, dynamic-page caveat, and quote limit
validation_gate: Local path exists and hash matches ledger.

intended_command_or_script: planned browser capture saves PNG outside Git under assets/private_use_screenshots/... and writes hash/log row
output_locations: data/source_snapshots/YYYY-MM-DD/ capture note; assets/private_use_screenshots/... ignored raster
required_hashes: sha256 of PNG; optional html_sha256; viewport; URL; page title; captured_at
ledger_updates: assets_manifest.tsv after capture; source_download_capture_log_i0183.tsv; optional screenshot queue row
robots_rights_note: Caption must say private-use/fair-use review pending unless rights cleared
validation_gate: No final layout use until raster hash, source URL, capture time, rights status, and blocked claims exist.

intended_command_or_script: planned render script outputs selected page images to ignored assets/... and writes dimensions/hash/page rows
output_locations: assets/gtc_2026/slides/ or assets/source_snapshots/YYYY-MM-DD/ ignored images; data rendered-page TSVs committed
required_hashes: sha256 of source PDF; sha256 of each rendered page; dimensions; page number
ledger_updates: assets_manifest.tsv for selected rendered pages; page notes TSV; claims.tsv only for specific supported claims
git_safety: Rendered PNG/JPG ignored; commit page metadata and captions only
robots_rights_note: Use as source-actor evidence; rights/fair-use review remains separate
validation_gate: Every rendered page row has source PDF hash, page number, image hash, dimensions, caption blocker.

intended_command_or_script: planned extractor reads captured snapshot and emits normalized TSV with snapshot_id/source_id/date/caveat fields
output_locations: data/*.tsv normalized table; data/source_snapshots/YYYY-MM-DD/ raw JSON/HTML when lightweight
required_hashes: sha256 of raw capture; optional hash of normalized table in note
ledger_updates: claims.tsv only after row-level normalization; chart data TSVs cite snapshot_id
git_safety: Commit normalized TSV; keep raw heavyweight/API dumps out if too large
robots_rights_note: No row can imply live rank/current price unless access date and scope are explicit
validation_gate: Every normalized row cites snapshot_id, source_id, metric definition, scope, and blocker.

intended_command_or_script: planned review script/table checks quote_limit_words, rights_status, source_url, and blocked_claims before promotion
required_hashes: n/a for review rows; source hashes cited by ID
ledger_updates: assets_manifest.tsv rights_or_private_use_note; claims.tsv caveat if claim promoted
git_safety: Review rows are lightweight and committed; no copyrighted bulk text copied
robots_rights_note: Direct quotes default to <=25 words/source and paraphrase fallback is recorded
validation_gate: No quote card or screenshot enters final top-100 without review row.

intended_command_or_script: planned manifest sync emits draft asset rows from capture logs for human/agent review
required_hashes: reuse capture hashes; no duplicate binary hash fields in manifest if log row has them
git_safety: Commit manifest rows; do not stage ignored rasters with git add -f unless user explicitly changes rules
robots_rights_note: Rights/private-use note required for every visual row
validation_gate: Field audit: caption, source_url_or_path, creator, accessed_date, story_purpose, rights/private-use note.

intended_command_or_script: planned check lists staged files over size/type threshold and fails on ignored heavy extensions
ledger_updates: commit message references pass ID and file list
git_safety: Existing .gitignore blocks PDFs, images, archives, audio, video, caches, rendered output; keep it that way
robots_rights_note: If a heavy file is needed, record path/hash and rationale, not binary
validation_gate: Before commit, status shows only TSV/MD/SVG/lightweight sources intended for pass.

intended_command_or_script: planned script writes rejected/blocked rows instead of silently skipping failures
required_hashes: sha256 of capture note if no source capture exists
ledger_updates: source_snapshot_protocol.tsv status capture_blocked/rejected/superseded; claims not promoted
git_safety: Blocked notes are committed as evidence; no retry loops that hide failures
robots_rights_note: Record 403, paywall, robots, binary payload, account requirement, or dynamic render caveat
validation_gate: Blocked capture still leaves auditable queue/log row and does not support new claims.
